title: Typography primitives for React content
description: Heading, Text, List, Blockquote, and code typography primitives for React and Next.js — semantic HTML, Tailwind-backed tones, and copy-ready previews.

og.title: Typography · Zentauri UI

twitter.title: Typography · Zentauri UI
twitter.description: Heading, Text, List, Blockquote, and code typography primitives for React and Next.js — semantic HTML, Tailwind-backed tones, and copy-ready previews.

intro: Browse headings, body copy, lists, quotations, inline emphasis, and code blocks from one hub. Each section demonstrates variants, formatting combinations, and snippets you can paste into App Router pages.
useCases: Marketing pages with consistent heading scale.
Documentation sites with lists and block quotes.
Dashboard copy with muted secondary lines.
Inline code and fenced samples without bespoke CSS.

faqs.question: Does Typography work with Next.js App Router?
faqs.answer: Yes. Primitives are plain React components; use them in server components unless you wrap interactive playgrounds that rely on client hooks.

faqs.question: Can I override styles with Tailwind?
faqs.answer: Pass className to merge utilities; tones map to semantic Tailwind text colors aligned with the rest of Zentauri UI.

faqs.question: How do I choose heading levels for accessibility?
faqs.answer: Match Heading level to document outline: one h1 per page where appropriate, then descend without skipping ranks inside each region.

sections.heading: What it does
sections.body:
Provides Heading, Text, List, Blockquote, InlineCode, and CodeBlock with shared tone and emphasis props.

Use semantic HTML by default and tune visuals with display overrides only when necessary.

sections.heading: Composition and API
sections.body:
Prefer small primitives over prop-heavy wrappers.

Compose bold, italic, underline, and strikethrough via booleans on Heading and Text.

sections.heading: Accessibility
sections.body:
Reserve headings for titles and sections.

Keep contrast sufficient for muted tones against your background; validate blockquotes with readable citations.

sections.heading: Next.js integration notes
sections.body:
Import from `@zentauri-ui/zentauri-components/ui/typography`.

Set NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL so canonical metadata resolves on deploy.
